narrative: Five o’clock and the sky has that heavy bruised look that anyone in Billings knows means snow. The weather service posts the warning at 4:47 and by five the grocery store parking lots are filling up with people who should have gone yesterday. Marcus is back at the refinery for his Friday shift and something has gone sideways — the safety report he filed two weeks ago about the number four cooling unit never made it past his shift supervisor, and he found out today because the unit tripped again. The argument in the break room is quiet, controlled, and worse for it: Marcus doesn’t raise his voice, which makes the supervisor raise his. Cassidy is setting up at The Rail for what she expects will be a big night — Friday plus incoming storm means everyone who was going to stay home will come out instead, the logic of Billings drinking culture being that bad weather is a social event. Cole is doing the thing he does before every storm: checking the south fence line, counting heads, making sure the generator has fuel.
events: Marcus gets into a heated argument with a shift supervisor at the refinery over a safety report he filed that was quietly shelved
A late-season snowstorm warning is issued for overnight — six to ten inches expected
